creator: Rebecca Bellan
title: (No AI) Synthesia’s AI training platform is moving beyond videos into live coaching
dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
content: Synthesia launched AI Roleplay Sessions, an interactive enterprise training platform where employees practice workplace conversations with AI avatars that provide feedback, scoring, and analytics to help companies measure training effectiveness.
contentSnippet: Synthesia launched AI Roleplay Sessions, an interactive enterprise training platform where employees practice workplace conversations with AI avatars that provide feedback, scoring, and analytics to help companies measure training effectiveness.

title: (No AI) Utility companies are promising to spare us from AI’s energy bill
author: Jess Weatherbed
content:
![Photo collage of a data center with data visualizations.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/04/STKS528_DATA_CENTERS_B.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

The pledge was introduced in March, and has done little to quell concerns so far. | Image: Cath Virginia / The Verge, Getty Images

In the face of backlash to concerns the AI boom will increase consumer electricity bills, the largest utility companies and data center developers in the US are now promising to do something about it. [_The Wall Street Journal_](https://www.wsj.com/politics/policy/trump-pledge-to-limit-ai-driven-electric-bill-increases-attracts-big-utilities-ae408981) reports that nearly 200 organizations have signed President Donald Trump's "rate payer protection pledge" that's meant to safeguard consumers from footing the AI bill.

Trump is expected to announce the new signatories on Thursday, which includes NextEra Energy, Duke Energy, Equinix, and Digital Realty, according to a list obtained by _The WSJ_. An unnamed White House official told the publication that those who have comm …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/969137/us-utility-ai-electricty-data-center-rate-pledge-trump)
contentSnippet:
The pledge was introduced in March, and has done little to quell concerns so far. | Image: Cath Virginia / The Verge, Getty Images	

In the face of backlash to concerns the AI boom will increase consumer electricity bills, the largest utility companies and data center developers in the US are now promising to do something about it. The Wall Street Journal reports that nearly 200 organizations have signed President Donald Trump's "rate payer protection pledge"  that's meant to safeguard consumers from footing the AI bill.
Trump is expected to announce the new signatories on Thursday, which includes NextEra Energy, Duke Energy, Equinix, and Digital Realty, according to a list obtained by The WSJ. An unnamed White House official told the publication that those who have comm …
Read the full story at The Verge.

The Wall Street Journal reports that nearly 200 organizations have signed President Donald Trump's "rate payer protection pledge" that's meant to […]

title: (No AI) Neill Blomkamp’s new zombie AI ‘film’ is just slop warmed over
author: Charles Pulliam-Moore
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/Screenshot-2026-07-21-at-12.22.06-PM.png?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

On Monday, _District 9_ and [_Gran Turismo_](https://www.theverge.com/23707049/sony-gran-turismo-movie-trailer) director Neill Blomkamp unveiled his latest project: a 13-minute sci-fi short titled _Nightborne_ that's loosely based on Peter Watts' 2014 novel _Echopraxia_. The short comes from Blomkamp's new AI startup / production company, Barley Studios, and features characters whose voices and faces are modeled after human actors. But every single one of _Nightborne_'s shots was made with ByteDance's [Seedance 2.0 text-to-video generator](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/877931/bytedance-seedance-2-video-generator-ai-launch).

In [an X post about the short](https://x.com/NeillBlomkamp/status/2079078300660769193), Blomkamp described it as a "test start" meant to demonstrate what generative AI is capable of, and he said that he wants "to tackle a full feature in th …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/entertainment/968703/neill-blomkamps-nightborne-barley-studios-seedance)
contentSnippet:
On Monday, District 9 and Gran Turismo director Neill Blomkamp unveiled his latest project: a 13-minute sci-fi short titled Nightborne that's loosely based on Peter Watts' 2014 novel Echopraxia. The short comes from Blomkamp's new AI startup / production company, Barley Studios, and features characters whose voices and faces are modeled after human actors. But every single one of Nightborne's shots was made with ByteDance's Seedance 2.0 text-to-video generator.
In an X post about the short, Blomkamp described it as a "test start" meant to demonstrate what generative AI is capable of, and he said that he wants "to tackle a full feature in th …
Read the full story at The Verge.

The short comes from Blomkamp's new AI startup / production company, Barley Studios, and features characters whose voices and faces are modeled after human actors. But […]

creator: Lily Hay Newman, Dell Cameron
title: (No AI) OpenAI Models Escaped Containment and Hacked Hugging Face
dc:creator: Lily Hay Newman, Dell Cameron
content: The cybersecurity-focused models, including GPT-5.6 Sol, broke out of a testing sandbox, exploited a zero-day, and gained access to the open internet to pull off the attack.
